us-politics.headline: Swalwell Faces New Sexual Misconduct Allegations, Resigns
us-politics.body: Democratic Rep. Eric Swalwell has resigned from Congress amid escalating sexual assault allegations. Multiple sources report new claims of misconduct, forcing Democrats into damage control mode. Party leaders are distancing themselves from Swalwell, with some calling for a full investigation into the accusations.

sources.title: Dems race to distance themselves from Swalwell amid allegations of rape, assault
How Eric Swalwell rose to the top of Democratic politics as rumors followed him
Eric Swalwell facing new sexual assault allegations after resignation from Congress
How Congress' workplace culture made accountability elusive
Miller on Swalwell: Dems have ‘blackmail files’ for when it suits them

us-politics.headline: Trump Escalates Tensions with Iran, Deploys More Troops
us-politics.body: The White House is ramping up military pressure on Iran, sending thousands of additional troops to the Middle East. U.S. naval forces have already blocked six Iranian ships in the Hormuz Strait. Congressional Republicans remain divided on war funding, with some supporting the president's aggressive stance and others expressing reservations.

sources.title: Senate GOP divided over Trump push for Iran war funding
War powers vote will test Senate’s support for Trump’s war with Iran
U.S. sends thousands more troops to Mideast as Trump seeks to squeeze Iran
Iranian sea trade blocked as six ships forced to turn back, U.S. military says

us-politics.headline: Crypto Bill and Tech Policy Take Center Stage in Congress
us-politics.body: The White House is pushing hard for new cryptocurrency legislation as Congress returns from recess. Simultaneously, debates intensify around technology in schools - with growing momentum to limit personal device use in classrooms. The emerging tech policy landscape reflects broader tensions around digital regulation and student screen time.

sources.title: White House ramps up pressure to pass crypto bill as Congress returns
Screen time opponents target 1-on-1 devices in schools after success with cellphone bans
In this U.S. hot spot for data centers, voters have turned against them

au-politics.headline: Political Donations Void as High Court Strikes Down Victoria Law
au-politics.body: Victoria faces a legal vacuum in political funding after the High Court ruled its donation laws unconstitutional. The decision leaves the state without limits on campaign contributions - a potential watershed moment for electoral financing. Political parties now operate in uncharted regulatory territory, with implications for transparency and potential influence in future elections.

sources.title: Victoria left without limits on political donations after high court rules laws unconstitutional

au-politics.headline: Rinehart Family Feud Continues Despite Son's Olive Branch
au-politics.body: Gina Rinehart's ongoing family legal battle took another turn with her son extending a conciliatory gesture. The long-running court case has exposed deep rifts within one of Australia's most prominent mining dynasties. While the son expressed a desire for family unity, the underlying tensions remain unresolved - highlighting the complex personal and financial dynamics at play.

sources.title: Who is the rival family pitted against Gina Rinehart in a long-running court case – and is she still the richest person in Australia?
Gina Rinehart’s son says he wants to be a ‘united family’ in olive branch to mother after court ruling
Here's what you need to know about today's Gina Rinehart court ruling

au-politics.headline: Labor Attacks Coalition's Immigration Plan as Divisive
au-politics.body: The Coalition's immigration policy has sparked heated debate, with Labor and cricket star Usman Khawaja condemning the proposed approach. The plan has been characterized as potentially discriminatory and impractical. Political lines are sharply drawn - with the government arguing the policy could harm Australia's multicultural fabric and social cohesion.

sources.title: Labor lashes Coalition immigration plan as Usman Khawaja calls policy ‘appalling’
Taylor's migration plan doesn't make much sense. It doesn't have to
Usman Khawaja condemns ‘appalling’ Coalition immigration comments – as it happened

world.headline: Iran-US Tensions Escalate as War Enters Critical Phase
world.body:
The ongoing conflict between Iran and the United States has reached a critical juncture. US military sources claim a complete blockade of Iranian ports has halted economic trade - a devastating blow to Iran's economy. The Iranian government now demands $270 billion in war reparations as diplomatic talks loom on the horizon.

Iran has also reported significant losses, including a recent incident where over 200 sailors were stranded after a US torpedo attack. The human and economic toll continues to mount, with both sides showing no signs of immediate de-escalation.

sources.title: More than 200 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack return home
Iran war: What is happening on day 47 of the US-Iran conflict?
Iran says $270bn war loss must be compensated, as fresh talks with US loom
US military says blockade of Iran ports ‘completely’ halts economic trade

world.headline: Global Diplomatic Shifts Emerge Amid Ongoing Conflicts
world.body:
International relations are experiencing significant turbulence. Italy has suspended its long-standing defense agreement with Israel - a move that signals growing diplomatic tensions. Meanwhile, South Africa has appointed a former apartheid-era negotiator as its US ambassador - a controversial choice that highlights complex political transitions.

The geopolitical landscape continues to shift, with China's President Xi meeting Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov and reaffirming their relationship as particularly precious. These diplomatic maneuvers suggest a complex global realignment in progress.

sources.title: Italy suspends long-standing defence agreement with Israel
South Africa names apartheid-era politician as new ambassador to the US
China’s Xi meets Russian FM Lavrov, calls relations with Moscow ‘precious’
South Africa appoints former apartheid-era negotiator as US ambassador

ai.headline: AI Ethics Crisis: Deepfake Nudes Terrorize School Communities
ai.body:
A global epidemic of AI-generated explicit images is devastating school communities. Wired reports the deepfake nude crisis has reached unprecedented levels - targeting students with synthesized pornographic content that causes severe psychological trauma and social disruption.

The problem extends far beyond isolated incidents. Schools struggle to respond, with existing legal frameworks failing to protect victims. Technology that was once theoretical has become a weaponized tool for harassment, raising urgent questions about AI regulation and digital consent.

sources.title: The Deepfake Nudes Crisis in Schools Is Much Worse Than You Thought
Building trust in the AI era with privacy-led UX

ai.headline: Tech Giants Expand AI and Satellite Capabilities Worldwide
ai.body:
Amazon is set to acquire Globalstar, dramatically expanding its satellite network infrastructure. The move signals intensifying competition in global connectivity - with implications for internet access and technological sovereignty.

Parallel developments show AI integration accelerating. Google's Gemma 4 now runs natively on iPhones with full offline inference, while Apple has selected Amazon's satellite network for device connectivity. These advances suggest a near future where AI and global communication technologies become seamlessly integrated.

sources.title: Amazon to acquire Globalstar and expand Amazon Leo satellite network
Google Gemma 4 Runs Natively on iPhone with Full Offline AI Inference
Apple chooses Amazon satellites for iPhone, years after rejecting Starlink offer
Google introduces "Skills" in Chrome to make Gemini prompts instantly reusable

ai.headline: AI Startups Reshape Healthcare and Corporate Dynamics
ai.body:
Anthropic's rapid rise is causing investor uncertainty in the AI landscape. The company's growing influence - including briefings with political administration - signals a shifting power dynamic among AI developers.

In healthcare, AI continues transforming patient interactions. Hospitals are increasingly deploying chatbots, while startups like MDalgorithms are expanding their technological footprint. The trend points to a future where AI becomes a primary interface for medical information and support.

sources.title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
Americans ask AI for health care. Hospitals think the answer is more chatbots.
Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
MDalgorithms (AI Healthcare) – Hiring Growth Marketer – Remote – $80K-$140K

btc.headline: Bitcoin ETFs Surge as Goldman Sachs Enters Market
btc.body: Bitcoin exchange-traded funds are drawing massive institutional interest, with $412 million in fresh inflows reported today. Goldman Sachs has filed to launch its own Bitcoin ETF, signaling growing mainstream financial acceptance of the cryptocurrency. The move suggests Wall Street's continued confidence in Bitcoin as an investment asset, despite ongoing market volatility.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETFs post $412M in inflows as Goldman Sachs files for BTC ETF
Iran conflict hints Bitcoin’s addressable market could exceed gold: Bitwise

btc.headline: Bitcoin Developers Battle Quantum Computing Threat
btc.body: Bitcoin's core developers are racing to protect the network from potential quantum computing attacks. Researchers have proposed BIP-361 - a radical plan to freeze coins vulnerable to quantum decryption. The proposed solution could force some Bitcoin holders to migrate their holdings, potentially disrupting the cryptocurrency's ecosystem. Quantum threats represent an existential challenge to Bitcoin's long-term security infrastructure.

sources.title: Bitcoin developers are trying to build quantum defenses. Your coins could pay the price.
Bitcoiners propose freezing quantum-vulnerable coins in BIP-361

btc.headline: Bitcoin Price Targets $90K as Bullish Signals Emerge
btc.body: Bitcoin is displaying strong bullish market behavior, with technical analysts targeting a potential $90,000 price point. The cryptocurrency has maintained support above $74,000 despite recent geopolitical tensions. Market sentiment remains optimistic, with some analysts seeing Bitcoin's addressable market potentially exceeding traditional safe-haven assets like gold.

sources.title: Iran conflict hints Bitcoin’s addressable market could exceed gold: Bitwise
Bitcoin shows ‘bull market behavior’ as chart pattern targets $90K
